fib.t1: Grow the stack frame from 32 bytes to 48 bytes. The three saved registers must sit at the top of the new frame: the return address at offset 40, s0 at offset 32, s1 at offset 24. Nothing else about the function's behavior may change.
fib.t2: Swap the roles of registers s0 and s1 throughout the function body: wherever the body used s0 it must now use s1, and vice versa. The prologue and epilogue must still save and restore both registers, each to/from its existing stack slot. Behavior must be unchanged.
fib.t3: Stop using s1 to carry the first recursive call's result. Instead: immediately after the first call, store a0 to the stack at offset 0 from sp; immediately before the final add, load that saved value into t2; the final add must then use t2 instead of s1. Remove s1's save and restore (and any related declarations), keeping the frame size at 32 bytes. Behavior must be unchanged.

quicksort.q1: Grow the stack frame from 32 bytes to 48 bytes. The four saved registers must sit at the top of the new frame: the return address at offset 40, s0 at offset 32, s1 at offset 24, s2 at offset 16. Nothing else about the function's behavior may change.
quicksort.q2: Swap the roles of registers s0 and s2 throughout the function body: wherever the body used s0 it must now use s2, and vice versa. The prologue and epilogue must still save and restore both registers, each to/from its existing stack slot. Behavior must be unchanged.
quicksort.q3: Currently the first recursive call relies on a1 still holding `low` (the second argument) from function entry. Instead, carry low in callee-saved s3 for the whole function: copy a1 into s3 immediately after the prologue's register saves, replace every later use of a1-as-low with s3, and explicitly set a1 from s3 immediately before the first recursive call. Save and restore s3 through a new stack slot: grow the frame from 32 to 48 bytes, keep the existing four slots at their current offsets, and put the new s3 slot at offset 32. Behavior must be unchanged.
quicksort.q4: Move the swap code (the block that exchanges array[boundary] and array[scan]) so it is laid out at the very end of the function, after the epilogue's return. Adjust branches, jumps, and fall-through edges as needed so behavior is unchanged.
quicksort.q5: Invert the element test in the partition body: make the conditional branch be taken when the element is less than or equal to the pivot (jumping to the swap code), with the fall-through path advancing the scan index. Reorder the swap and advance blocks as needed so every fall-through edge remains adjacent and behavior is unchanged.
